name: Validation and CI
summary: Makefile targets, validation scripts, smoke commands, contract drift checks, testing posture, and future CI wiring.
invariants: Validation failures must not be masked or claimed as pass.
Claims of success require the command to have passed in the current work unit or an explicit residual gap.
SwiftPM validation should be serialized to avoid build-lock contention.
Validation does not itself imply production hardening.
allowedWork: Maintain validation scripts, Makefile targets, smoke targets, and drift checks.
Add CI wiring when scoped by a validation/CI task.
Record exact failing commands and classify whether failures are caused by the current work.
Strengthen checks without weakening doctrine or runtime boundaries.
forbiddenWork: Mask failures with broad `|| true` validation commands.
Delete or weaken drift checks to make unrelated work green.
Run concurrent Swift builds that contend on `.build` locks.
knownGaps: GitHub Actions CI wiring remains a separate READY product/repo task.
Some validation commands may be expensive and should still be reported honestly.
notes: Use diagnostic grep exceptions only where no-match is acceptable and explicitly diagnostic.
Territory records do not replace actual command execution.
